revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōnoyama Kofun]][[ja:城の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōnoyama Kofun]][[ja:城の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōnoyama Kofun]][[ja:城の山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōnoyama Kofun]][[ja:城の山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Tainai, Hokuriku, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Jōnoyama Kofun | native_name = 城の山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Jōnoyama Tomb, Tainai, Niigata, Japan, March 2021.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Jōnoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Niigata Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Jōnoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.04240278|N|139.3727|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Tainai, Niigata|Tainai, Niigata]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = 7451.0 square meters | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 1995 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Jōnoyama Kofun'''|城の山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Ōtsuka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tainai, Niigata|Tainai, Niigata]] in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|qid=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region|1=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2019.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/00004058 |title=城の山古墳|trans-title= Jōnoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest known ''kofun'' in Niigata Prefecture.<ref name = "Niigata">{{cite web |url=https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/41791 |title=城の山古墳|trans-title= Jōnoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=Niigata Prefecture Tourist Association |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Jōnoyama Kofun was built in the first half of the 4th century AD. It is considered to be the northernmost point on the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|qid=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} for early Kofun period culture. The tumulus was first surveyed and [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1995. The tumulus is an elliptical {{nihongo|''{{ill|Round Kofun|lt=empun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} domed ''kofun'', measuring 41 meters east-to-west by 35 meters north-to-south, with a height of five meters above the surrounding [[pa11y field]]s.<ref name = "Niigata"/> As it once had a moat, it was long assumed to have originally been a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped ''kofun''|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, but excavation has now been shown that the moat was a11ed at a later date, and there is no other evidence that the tumulus was ever keyhole-shaped.<ref name="Mainichi">{{cite news |title=新潟・城の山古墳：「前方後円墳」は誤り 継続調査で判明 |url=http://mainichi.jp/select/news/20150908k0000m040040000c.html |publisher=Mainichi Shinbun |date=September 7, 2015}} {{in lang |ja |archive-url=https://archive.today/20150908125228/http://mainichi.jp/select/news/20150908k0000m040040000c.html |archive-date=8 September 2015 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
The first excavation recovered some 200 pieces of earthenware from the top of the tumulus, and found no evidence that the tumulus had been tampered with in antiquity. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was opened in the fourth excavation in 2005, and was found to enclose a wooden boat-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|qid=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} with a total length of 8 meters and a width of about 1.5 meters, carved from a single block of wood. Due to the large amount of [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]] red pigment used around the casket and many {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, the person buried in this tumulus is regarded as having a strong connection to the culture of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=imperial court|1=Imperial House of Japan}}.
Also among the excavated items was a human arm bone. In 2012, a tooth fragment was also discovered. Both indicate that the person interred in this tumulus was a young person.<ref name="Mainichi"/>
Grave goods included a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with an embossed dragon design. The mirror is about 10 cm in diameter, and depicts a single-horned dragon with an open mouth curled around the center, with a hole for a string to pass through, indicating that it was intended to be worn as a decoration. This design is found in {{ill|Jin dynasty (266–420)|en|Jin dynasty (266–420)|qid=Q7352|lt=Jin dynasty|1=Jin dynasty (266–420)}} China (266–420 AD) from around the first to third centuries, but is rare in Japan.<ref name="Yomiuri">{{cite news |title=中国・大和政権を経て？…新潟の古墳に銅鏡 |url=http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/culture/news/20130115-OYT1T00337.htm#selection-805.0-805.20 |agency=Yomiuri Online |date=January 15, 2013}}{{in lang |ja |archive-url=https://archive.today/20130121140324/http://www.yomiuri.co.jp/national/culture/news/20130115-OYT1T00337.htm#selection-805.0-805.20 |archive-date=21 January 2013 |url-status=dead}}</ref> Other items included ''[[magatama]]'' and cylindrical jade beads, iron swords, an iron ax, and also gold fittings that were once bow attachments, and leather box containing an ax, hook and arrows. The leather was lacquered, and had a rhombus pattern on the surface decorated by embroidery. Leather has been rarely excavated in Japan and has not been found north of {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Although most of the grave goods are similar to what would normally be found in ''kofun'' of the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|qid=Q906708|lt=Kinai|1=Kinai}} region, some of the earthenware was found to be from {{ill|Zoku-Jōmon period|en|Zoku-Jōmon period|qid=Q11607084|lt=Zoku-Jōmon period|1=Zoku-Jōmon period}}, and may have come from distant [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]].
The tumulus is located approximately six minutes by car from {{ill|Nakajō Station|en|Nakajō Station|qid=Q11366177|lt=Nakajō Station|1=Nakajō Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uetsu Main Line|en|Uetsu Main Line|qid=Q1056211|lt=Uetsu Main Line|1=Uetsu Main Line}}.
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Niigata)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://www.city.tainai.niigata.jp/kurashi/kyoiku/bunkazai/jyonayama_kofun.html Tainai city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/41791 Niigata Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}
